name: openmbd_female
citation: Tierney. OpenMBD: An Open-Source Multibody Dynamics Simulator for Biomechanics Research and Education. F1000Research, 2026.
description: Open-source multibody dynamics model, adult female

Abdomen.description: Anterior trunk between pelvis and rib cage. Ellipsoids represent paired rectus abdominis, external obliques, and lower abdominal wall. Contact stiffness from Kroell et al. (1971) [9] scaled to regional soft-tissue area.

metadata.name: OpenMBD Female Model
metadata.derivation: Derived from openmbd_male.json. Segment masses per de Leva (1996) female mass fractions relative to total body mass (77.9 kg, CDC NHANES 2021-2023); trunk residual mass distributed using male internal trunk proportions. All segment masses normalised to sum exactly to 77.9 kg (normalisation factor 0.9745, accounting for de Leva fractions summing to 102.6% when clavicle and neck are included). Inertia tensors scaled by segment mass ratio and height-ratio squared (H_f/H_m = 0.9206). Shoulder geometry narrowed by factor 0.893 (biacromial ratio, CDC-NHANES/ANSUR). Hip geometry widened by factor 1.069 (sitting hip breadth ratio, ANSUR). Lower-limb ellipsoid radii scaled by cube-root of de Leva (1996) female/male mass fraction ratio. Biacromial and biiliac width scaling per Gordon et al. (1989).
